task: I need to send a short note to a client about a delay, and I want to make sure it covers the key point without creating extra back-and-forth. Can you help me draft a simple message that gets the update across clearly?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a simple version you can use:

**Subject:** Update on Timing

Hi [Client Name],

I wanted to let you know there is a delay on our side, and the timing will need to shift. We’re working to resolve it as quickly as possible and will keep you updated as soon as we have more information.

Please let me know if you’d like to discuss this further.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also make it:
- a little more firm
- a little warmer
- or shorter, like a one-paragraph email

user_simulator.text:
Close, but I’d want it a little tighter and less open-ended. The “please let me know if you’d like to discuss this further” part invites back-and-forth I don’t need. I’d rather have something that states the delay, gives a brief reason if needed, and says I’ll follow up when there’s a concrete update.

Something like:

Hi [Client Name],

Quick update: there is a delay on my side, and I need a bit more time to complete this. I’m working through it and will send the next update as soon as I have a firm timeline.

Best,
[Your Name]

If you want, make it even shorter and a little more direct.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter, more direct version:

Hi [Client Name],

Quick update: there is a delay on my side, and I need a bit more time to complete this. I’m working through it and will send the next update as soon as I have a firm timeline.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can make it:
- even shorter
- a little firmer
- or slightly warmer while still staying concise

user_simulator.text: That works. Tight enough, no extra invitation to chase it. I’d use it as-is.
